name: Time of Day
description: Session-aware model that trades repeatable opening-drive, sweep, or reversion behavior inside defined intraday windows.

context: Exact session clock window defined before the session opens.
Asia high and low, London open, New York open, and prior session extremes mapped.
Window classified in advance as breakout or reversion.
setup: Price reaching the planned level or range inside the target window.
Liquidity sweep or opening drive forming on time.
Session behavior matching the repeatable intraday pattern.
trigger: Opening range break for expansion setups.
Reclaim of the swept level for reversal setups.
Execution occurring inside the active session window.
confirmation: Volume and range expanding during the planned window.
Price holding on the correct side of the session level after trigger.
No conflicting macro release due in the immediate execution window.
invalidation: Acceptance on the wrong side of the key session level.
Opening range direction flipping and holding against the trade.
noTrade: Setup driven by one-off news randomness instead of repeatable session behavior.
Spread and slippage elevated around the session open.

stopLoss: Protective stop beyond the session level or sweep extreme.
Tighter stop structure for open-drive setups and wider structure stop for sweep reclaims.
Invalidation distance remaining inside planned session volatility.
takeProfit: Primary target at the opposite side of the opening range, ADR objective, or prior session extreme.
Runner valid only while session momentum remains intact.
timeExit: Position closed by the end of the planned session window.
Open-drive setup flattened before lunch or rollover.
Trade closed before liquidity dies if price stalls.
earlyExit: Session level failing immediately after entry.
Price unable to hold above or below the opening range.
